_comment: Compliant Apify Proxy policy for Ex-Ditector (Self Footprint Audit Pro). This declares HOW proxies may be used: for AVAILABILITY/reliability only (geo-correct rendering of a user's OWN public page, IP diversity to be a polite client) and NEVER for EVASION (defeating bans, rate limits, captcha, or login walls). Every field here is read by proxy-policy.js and re-checked against the scope gate at request time. NO credentials live in this file; APIFY_PROXY_PASSWORD / APIFY_TOKEN are supplied by the operator at deploy time. This product asserts NO live deployment.
_intent_doc: availability_only = proxy chosen for IP/geo diversity and reliable fetching of public/self pages. The ONLY legitimate reason. The opposite, 'evasion', is categorically refused by proxy-policy.js (see refusal codes).

DATACENTER.why: Fastest, cheapest, lowest footprint. Default tier for fetching a user's own public page. Apify auto-rotation gives polite IP diversity without targeting anti-bot defenses.

RESIDENTIAL.why: Allowed ONLY when geo-accurate rendering matters (a public page that varies by country for the user's own locale). NOT a means to look 'more human' to defeat a block. Requires geo_justification and a real country_code; never used to churn through a ban.

denied_groups.GOOGLE_SERP: Out of product scope; we audit a named subject's own/public sources, not scrape search engines at volume.

geo._doc: Country targeting is for AVAILABILITY (render the user's own page as their region sees it), not for masquerading. subdivision (US state) targeting is intentionally NOT enabled — finer geo serves no availability purpose here and edges toward profiling.

session._doc: Sessions exist for transport stability (sticky IP for a coherent fetch), mirroring Crawlee's SessionPool. They are NOT a rotation weapon to outlast a ban. retire_on_block below is fail-closed: a block STOPS the subject, it does not trigger a fresh-IP retry loop.

compliance_floor._retire_doc: Crawlee retires a session on 401/403/429 and rotates to a fresh IP to KEEP scraping. We invert the intent: a 401/403/429/503 is read as 'this resource does not want this client right now' => COMPLIANCE STOP for that subject (hand to human review), NOT a fresh-IP retry. Same mechanism (detect block), opposite reflex (stop, not evade).
compliance_floor._retries_doc: At most ONE retry, and ONLY for genuine transport faults (ECONNRESET / socket hangup / timeout) where the response carried no block signal. A block status NEVER counts as a retryable transport fault.

_residential_doc: Residential proxy is a dual-use capability (it is the tier people reach for to look human). So it is gated to the SAME chokepoint as other dual-use techniques in this product: scope=self|public_figure only, and only with a geo_justification. consented/brand/safety_evidence get datacenter only.

env.APIFY_PROXY_PASSWORD: proxy password from the Apify account; used to build the proxy URL at run time. Unset => proxy-client.js DRY-RUNS and prints the redacted URL it WOULD build.
